entities.name: PIGEONPEA
entities.description: A legume crop with significant genetic diversity in Eastern and Southern Africa.

entities.name: SORGHUM
entities.description: A cereal crop with significant genetic diversity in Eastern and Southern Africa.

entities.name: FINGER MILLET
entities.description: A small-seeded cereal crop with significant genetic diversity in Eastern and Southern Africa.

entities.name: EASTERN AND SOUTHERN AFRICA
entities.description: A region serving as a center of genetic diversity for pigeonpea, sorghum, and finger millet.

entities.name: NAIROBI-KENYA
entities.description: Location of the ICRISAT regional bank maintaining germplasm accessions.

entities.name: ICRISAT-PATANCHERU
entities.description: Location where global genetic diversity is maintained.

entities.name: GERMPLASM
entities.description: Genetic resources maintained in banks and used for crop improvement.

entities.name: GENETIC DIVERSITY
entities.description: The total number of genetic characteristics in the genetic makeup of a species.

entities.name: BREEDING PROGRAM
entities.description: A systematic approach to improving crop traits using local germplasm.

entities.name: GENETIC ENHANCEMENT PROGRAM
entities.description: A region-specific program focused on improving specific crop traits.

entities.name: DROUGHT TOLERANCE
entities.description: The ability of a crop to withstand water-deficient conditions.

entities.name: HIGH GRAIN YIELD
entities.description: The capacity of a crop to produce a large amount of grain per unit area.

entities.name: MATURITY
entities.description: The time taken for a crop to reach full development, categorized as short, medium, or long.

entities.name: INTER-CROPPING COMPATIBILITY
entities.description: The ability of a crop to be grown successfully alongside another crop.

entities.name: PHOTOPERIOD INSENSITIVITY
entities.description: The ability of a crop to flower regardless of the day length.

entities.name: GRAIN QUALITY
entities.description: The physical and chemical characteristics of the grain preferred by farmers and consumers.

entities.name: FUSARIUM WILT RESISTANCE
entities.description: The ability of a crop to resist infection by Fusarium wilt.

entities.name: FUSARIUM WILT
entities.description: A fungal disease affecting crops.

entities.name: HELICOVERPA POD BORER RESISTANCE
entities.description: The ability of a crop to resist attack by the Helicoverpa pod borer.

entities.name: HELICOVERPA POD BORER
entities.description: An insect pest that attacks crop pods.

entities.name: CLIMATE CHANGE RESILIENCE
entities.description: The capacity of a crop to recover from or adapt to climate change impacts.

entities.name: INSECT TOLERANCE
entities.description: The ability of a crop to withstand insect pest attacks.

entities.name: SEED SIZE
entities.description: The physical mass or size of the seeds, such as 28 g/100 seed mass.

entities.name: AGRO-ECOLOGICAL ADAPTATION
entities.description: The ability of a crop to thrive in specific local environmental conditions.
